fixed: 375px (medium): Sjakk-NM channel truncated to 'Direktesport…' — channel label now wraps, showing the full name + any qualifier
900px (medium): 'Direktesport (grati…' — channel-column truncation was width-independent; label now wraps to a quiet second line instead of ellipsis-clipping
375px (medium): Wimbledon channel truncated to 'HBO Max (fi…' — now wraps fully
900px (low): 'HBO Max (finale g…' resolved by the same wrap change
notes: Root cause: .ev-where used white-space:nowrap + overflow:hidden + text-overflow:ellipsis with a fixed max-width (116px desktop / 84px phone), so any channel label longer than the cap clipped regardless of available viewport width — hiding qualifiers like '(gratis)'.

Fix (CSS only, scoped to .ev-where): wrap the channel label at word boundaries (overflow-wrap:break-word, line-height 1.3) instead of clipping, so a qualifier drops to a quiet right-aligned second line. Desktop cap raised 116px→128px for extra breathing room.

Phone: kept the channel footprint at 84px (title must win the phone's width per the calm-design comment) but reduced channel font 12.5px→11.5px so the longest single channel word ('Direktesport') fits on one line without stealing title space. Verified 'eliteklassen' still wraps as a whole word — no title regression.

Verified at 375/393/900 with real data AND with '(gratis)'/'(finale gratis)' qualifiers temporarily injected to reproduce the exact finding (data reverted before commit). Layout stays a calm single column, no new overflow, header/agenda/disclosure/footer intact.
npm test: 173 passed. No data files touched.
